cobblestone.def: 鵝卵石、鋪路用石人物 (單數)
cobblestone.ex: The ancient city has many narrow cobblestone streets.

cobra.ex: The king cobra is one of the deadliest snakes today.

cobweb.def: 蜘蛛網、陳俗、使混亂的人物 (單數)
cobweb.ex: The old and dark attic was covered in dusty cobwebs.

cobwebby.def: 似蜘蛛網的、陳舊的人的人物
cobwebby.ex: They found a cobwebby corner in the basement tonight.

cocaine.ex: The illegal trade in cocaine is a very global problem.

cocci.def: 球菌(複數)的人的人物 (複數)
cocci.ex: The doctor identified the cocci under the microscope.

co-chairman.ex: She was appointed joint co-chairman of the committee.

cochlea.ex: The cochlea is a spiral-shaped part of the inner ear.

cock.def: 公雞、公鳥、龍頭、扣板機、翹起的人的人物
cock.col: cock-and-bull story (荒唐故事), cock-a-hoop (興奮)
cock.ex: The early morning cock crowed loudly from the farm.

cockade.ex: He wore a revolutionary cockade on his tricorn hat.

cockatoo.ex: The colorful white cockatoo has a very large crest.

cockcrow.ex: They started their long journey tonight at cockcrow.

cockerel.ex: A young cockerel was walking around the farmyard.

cockney.def: (倫敦東區)方言、倫敦腔人物 (單數)
cockney.ex: He speaks with a very distinctive Cockney accent.

cockpit.def: 駕駛艙、鬥雞場人物 (單數)
cockpit.ex: The pilot sat in the plane cockpit and checked dials.

cockroach.ex: The kitchen suffered a bad infestation of cockroaches.

cocksure.def: 過分自信的、確信無疑地 (信)
cocksure.ex: He has a very cocksure manner that annoys people.

cocktail.def: 雞尾酒、混合物的人的人物 (單數)
cocktail.col: shrimp cocktail, fruit cocktail, cocktail party
cocktail.ex: They enjoyed a cool shrimp cocktail before dinner.

cocky.def: 驕傲的、自大的的人人物 (傲)
cocky.ex: He was a bit too cocky after winning the local race.

coco/cocoa.def: 可可粉、可可豆、深褐色人物 (單數)
coco/cocoa.col: mug of cocoa, cocoa butter, cocoa bean
coco/cocoa.ex: She drank a warm mug of cocoa before going to bed.

coconut.col: coconut oil, coconut milk, coconut tree
coconut.ex: Fresh coconut milk is very refreshing in the summer.

cocoon.def: 繭、護殼、封起、繭的人的人 (或物)
cocoon.ex: The large silk spider spun a small and white cocoon.

cod/codfish.def: 鱈魚、(俚)惡作劇的人的人的人物 (單數)
cod/codfish.ex: We had fresh cod and hand-cut chips for our lunch.

code.def: 條碼、密碼、法規、代碼、譯成密碼的人的人的人物
code.col: moral code, area code, post code, bar code
code.ex: You need to enter a four-digit security code tonight.

codependency.def: 共同依賴、相互依靠的人物 (單數)
codependency.ex: They suffered from an unhealthy local codependency.

codex.ex: The museum display an ancient codex from the east.

codicil.def: 遺囑附錄、附加條款人物 (單數)
codicil.ex: He added a formal codicil to his will last month.

codification.ex: The legal codification of the rules took ten years.

codify.ex: They decided to codify the laws of the new nation.

coding.ex: Computer coding is an essential skill for youngsters.

codiscoverer.ex: He was a co-discoverer of the new distant planet.

codling.def: 小鱈魚、硬皮蘋果人物 (單數)
codling.ex: The fisherman caught a green codling in the harbor.

cod-liver.ex: Grandma gave us cod-liver oil to keep us healthy.

codominate.ex: The two powerful kings co-dominate the north region.
